title: Ageratina subpenninervia
intro: Ageratina subpenninervia, also known as 'Estramadura false ageratum', is a flowering plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina subpenninervia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, such as 14-deoxy-11, 12-dihydro-13-isoobtusifoliol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ageratina subpenninervia is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ageratina subpenninervia is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ageratina, which is a group of flowering plants. The species Ageratina subpenninervia is further categorized as a perennial herb native to Mexico. Its classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. Ageratina subpenninervia is also known by its synonym, Eupatorium subpenninervium.
uses: Ageratina subpenninervia, a species of flowering plant, has been studied for its potential medicinal uses.
